result.collection_after: 16738 tests collected, 11 errors (pre-existing, unrelated to launcher: mesh/auth/hle/clarification/perspective/playwright collection failures)

codex_points: Evidence is insufficient for strict behaviour-preservation approval: the oracle proves resolved config/command equivalence, NOT process-level invocation semantics of the shims.
What IS verified and accepted: env/profile dicts byte-identical, dynamic/by-path references intact, no in-use legacy path deleted, oracle byte-identical (sha 9c0a3d43...) across all 9 variants.
Core gap: old paths map to the 9 variants but end-to-end shim equivalence is never demonstrated - no legacy path was actually invoked end-to-end.
Test collection (16738 collected, 11 pre-existing unrelated errors) does not exercise launcher runtime behaviour, so it adds no runtime assurance.
To flip to SAFE, invoke all 9 legacy paths end-to-end and show preservation of: exact argv (quoting, empty args, `--`), exit codes, stdin/stdout/stderr/TTY behaviour.
Also must preserve: signal propagation (ideally via exec-style call-through), invocation from arbitrary working directories, launcher resolution without unsafe `$0`/`__file__` assumptions.
Also must preserve: environment inheritance/override/unset semantics, executable permissions, and shebang behaviour.
Any failure in those areas is externally observable and would make the consolidation non-preserving - hence the strict REVISE despite clean oracle/env evidence.
